perturbation.original_text: 1.8 "Dassault Systemes Customer(s)" means any customer of the Co-Branded Service having accessed such Web service, wherever hosted, by first logging in on any Dassault Systemes Web site.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.8 "Dassault Systemes Customer(s)" means any customer of the Co-Branded Service having accessed such Web service, wherever hosted, by first logging in on any Dassault Systemes Web site, and has completed at least one transaction within the past 30 days.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a requirement of recent transaction activity to the definition of 'Dassault Systemes Customer(s)'. This could conflict with Section 6.1 which obligates PlanetCAD to maintain and update Dassault Systemes' Customer Database(s) without explicitly stating the customer status has to be conditioned to recent transaction, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 6 DASSAULT SYSTEMES CUSTOMER DATABASES 6.1 PlanetCAD's obligation to maintain and update Dassault Systemes Customer Databases. For as long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, PlanetCAD will maintain and continuously update Dassault Systemes' Customer Database(s), which shall include without limitation, databases of Dassault Systemes Customers, of demands from and results of Web services supplied to Dassault Systemes Customers and associated Net Revenue and of data collected via the Customer Relations Management ("CRM") environment. The Dassault Systemes Customer Databases must be held separately from any other databases held or maintained by PlanetCAD. If not held separately, the Dassault Systemes Customer Databases must be clearly identified and sorted out as the property of Dassault Systemes and the provisions regarding PlanetCAD's limited right of use and its obligation of confidentiality as set forth below apply without change.
perturbation.justification: The modified text adds a 'recent transaction' requirement to the definition of 'Dassault Systemes Customer(s)', which directly contradicts Section 6.1's obligation for PlanetCAD to maintain and update Dassault Systemes' Customer Database(s) without any such condition. This introduces a clear inconsistency, as PlanetCAD might be obligated to maintain records for customers who no longer qualify under the revised definition, thus creating an ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 Share of Net Revenue. As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers who have not previously purchased services directly from PlanetCAD shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a distinction in revenue sharing based on whether a customer had a prior direct relationship with PlanetCAD. This contradicts the original clear apportionment rule, creating ambiguity about which customers and sales are subject to this adjusted revenue share, thus an in-text contradiction occurs. The impact could be on how net revenue is defined, where the original definition and how it is being shared is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 Net Revenue Defined. Net Revenue shall consist of all revenues recognized by the Billing Party derived from supply of the Co-Branded Service, including the price paid by the customers, less any applicable discounts and net of any commissions or fees paid to third party resellers, to obtain access to the Co-Branded Service and any other fees and charges invoiced by the Billing Party to the customers, without deduction by such party of any other costs or expenses related to achievement of the revenue, provided, however, that Net 7 8 Revenue shall be reduced by the amount, if any, of (i) value-added taxes, (ii) sales taxes or (iii) withholding taxes imposed by any jurisdiction on payments made by a payor in such jurisdiction to a payee outside of such jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The modification to section 5.4 introduces a new criterion for revenue sharing (previous customer relationship with PlanetCAD) that is not accounted for in the definition of 'Net Revenue' in section 5.5. Section 5.5 defines Net Revenue based on revenues recognized from the supply of the Co-Branded Service, without any exclusion based on prior customer relationships. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the application of the revenue sharing percentages depends on a factor not included in the Net Revenue definition. This ambiguity affects the calculation and distribution of Net Revenue, creating a contradiction in how revenue is apportioned and defined, which fits the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 Breach. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event that the other party is in default or breach of any material provision of this Agreement, and such default or breach continues unremedied for a period of sixty days after written notice thereof. In addition to the right to terminate this Agreement, the non-breaching party, shall have all rights and remedies available at law and in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 Breach. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time in the event that the other party is in default or breach of any material provision of this Agreement, and such default or breach continues unremedied for a period of sixty days after written notice thereof, unless the breaching party can demonstrate that the breach was due to circumstances beyond its reasonable control. In addition to the right to terminate this Agreement, the non-breaching party, shall have all rights and remedies available at law and in equity.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless the breaching party can demonstrate that the breach was due to circumstances beyond its reasonable control' creates an exception to the termination clause. This introduces subjectivity and potential disputes over what constitutes 'circumstances beyond its reasonable control,' creating uncertainty in enforcement. This impacts the definiteness of Section 8 Representations and Warranties, creating an in-text contradiction if this section of the contract requires specific and unwavering performance standards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SECTION 8 REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES 8.1 Mutual Representations. Each party represents and warrants to the other party as follows: (a) The performance of this Agreement does not infringe or conflict with any Intellectual Property right of any third party, and (ii) no confidential, proprietary or trade secret information of either party or their personnel that will be used in performing this Agreement has been misappropriated from any third party; and 10 11 (b) All services, work, obligations or assignments performed by either party under this Agreement will be of professional quality, conforming to generally accepted practices within the industry, and the Infrastructure including Third Party Software shall function and perform to the specifications and requirements thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the termination clause in Section 10.2, stating that termination is not allowed if the breach was due to circumstances beyond the breaching party's reasonable control. This directly contradicts Section 8.1(b), which warrants that all services and work will be of professional quality and conform to generally accepted practices, and the Infrastructure will function and perform to specifications. The added exception in Section 10.2 introduces ambiguity and potential for dispute, as it allows for non-performance under certain 'circumstances beyond reasonable control,' conflicting with the absolute warranty of performance in Section 8.1(b). This makes the enforcement of Section 8 uncertain and creates an in-text contradiction.
